title: AGENTS.md
text: erplots provides fluent mini-language building exposure-response plots: model curves/ribbons, quantile-binned response-rate/mean summaries, raw-data layer, grouped distribution panels. model-agnostic: erplots never fits model . model implementing er_predict() can visualised; implementing er_simulate() er_summary() additionally enables uncertainty spaghetti plots/VPCs summary annotations (e.g. p-values). See ?er_model_interface. er_plot() takes response_type = c("auto", "binary", "continuous", "count") argument (auto-detected response column given: logical values entirely {0, 1} → "binary", else "continuous"; "auto" never resolves "count" – must declared explicitly), stored object$response$type/ object$response$limits. model curve/ribbon (er_plot_add_model()) group panel (er_plot_add_groups()) layers work response type dispatch needed. quantile summary layer (er_plot_add_quantiles()) er_vpc_plot() now fully generalised across three response types (rate + Clopper-Pearson "binary"; mean + t-interval "continuous"; mean + exact Poisson interval "count"). data layer (er_plot_add_data(), renamed er_plot_show_datastrip()) also works response type, "continuous"/"count" error path left – see “Planned work” . (er_plot_add_data() went two renames: er_plot_show_datastrip() -> er_plot_show_data() -> current name, latter part naming-scheme review described “Extensibility” .) er_plot_add_data() offers two mutually exclusive structural families, selected builder function passed (separate style argument – see “Extensibility” ): default, er_builder_data_overlay(), plots raw observations true (exposure, response) coordinates directly model panel – single, response-type-agnostic builder small vertical jitter applied binary responses, color aesthetic ’s always strata (sharing base plot’s legend) rather response value. er_builder_data_boxjitter() (binary-response ) older panel-based design (object$part$data) – boxplot exposure values jittered points layered top, upper (responders)/lower (non-responders) panels, showing exposure distribution conditional response rather just raw points; fill means strata box color means strata points, mirroring model layer’s ribbon/line split. two structural families live separate object$part slots (overlay vs. data); passing builder one family clears slot. built-"panel"-layout builder continuous/count response – older build_data_jitter() (binary) build_data_color() (continuous/count) removed review found neither earned keep alongside er_builder_data_overlay()/er_builder_data_boxjitter() (see PLAN.md); custom "panel"-layout builder continuous/count remains possible via er_builder_tag(), since .part_data()’s response-type dispatch left place. er_builder_data_hex() second "overlay"-layout data builder alongside er_builder_data_overlay(): geom_hex()-based 2D density, N large enough raw points overplot unreadable smear (useful continuous/count responses, whose y-values spread rather piled 0/1). requires hexbin package (Suggests, guarded rlang::check_installed()). geom_hex()’s fill aesthetic already encodes bin density, ’s channel left color = strata; stratified, strata pooled single hex-binned density (rlang::inform() message noting ) rather partially misleadingly encoding strata. er_builder_data_hex() tags via er_builder_tag(builder, layout = "overlay", fill_role = "density") – pieces builder metadata set one call (mirroring er_builder_group_histogram()’s y_role tag) – .polish_labels() reads (via internal .builder_fill_role() accessor) title base plot’s fill legend “Count” rather strata label uses default. matters er_builder_data_hex()’s fill sole fill mapping base plot: stratified er_builder_model_ribbonline() also maps fill = strata (discrete) ribbon, ggplot2 can’t reconcile er_builder_data_hex()’s continuous density fill aesthetic – errors (“Continuous value supplied discrete scale”) rather silently misrendering. genuine ggplot2 limitation, fixable inside builder; pair stratified er_builder_data_hex() plot model builder doesn’t map fill, e.g. er_builder_model_line() (color ). group panel layer (er_plot_add_groups()) third built-builder, er_builder_group_histogram(), alongside er_builder_group_boxplot() (default) er_builder_group_violin(). Unlike two – put group variable y-axis (y = lvl, one categorical row per level) – histogram needs y-axis free counts, er_builder_group_histogram() puts group levels facet strips instead (facet_grid(rows = vars(lvl), switch = "y")) tags via er_builder_tag(builder, y_role = "count"), consolidated setter data layer’s layout/fill_role tags go . .polish_labels() reads tag (via internal .builder_y_role() accessor) title y-axis “Count” rather group variable’s label (still uses er_builder_group_boxplot()/ er_builder_group_violin(), group variable y-axis); builders don’t set tag keep old, categorical-label behaviour, opt-rather breaking requirement like layout . Putting group levels facet strips also surfaced second, unrelated quirk: ggplot2’s default text angle left-hand strip (switch = "y") rotated 90 degrees, sized fit (short) row height rather (longer) available width, long lvl labels like "Placebo (N=100)" getting clipped vertically. er_builder_group_histogram() works around theme(strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0)), rotating text back horizontal strip auto-expands fit full label. companion package erglm (formerly erlr) fits GLM-based exposure-response models implements package’s generics model objects. erplots hard dependency erglm – ’s listed Suggests, examples/tests/vignettes. Since erglm GitHub-(CRAN), DESCRIPTION also declares Remotes: djnavarro/erglm CI can resolve . Key API points remember: example dataset erglm::erglm_data (erlr::lr_data), models fit erglm::erglm_model(formula, data, family = ...) (family explicit). erglm now genuinely supports binomial/poisson/gaussian/Gamma families, matching binary (ae1/ae2), count (ae_count), continuous (biomarker_change, ae_duration) response columns erglm_data – just binomial. Models simulated erglm::erglm_vpc_sim(); er_predict()/er_simulate()/ er_summary() methods erglm registers model objects correct four families, erplots-side changes needed .

title: AGENTS.md
text: naming-scheme review (prompted build_* prefix reading unintuitive, CI helpers using inconsistent suffix) settled four families, prefix, documented future additions stay consistent: er_plot_add_*() – pipeline verbs add layer er_plot spec (er_plot_add_model(), er_plot_add_quantiles(), er_plot_add_data(), er_plot_add_groups()). Renamed er_plot_show_*(): “add” accurately signals “append spec” “show” , since nothing actually drawn er_plot_build()/print()/plot(). er_plot(), er_plot_style(), er_plot_build() keep bare er_plot_* name – aren’t layer-adders. er_builder_*() – pluggable partial-builder functions (build_*()), e.g. er_builder_model_ribbonline(), er_builder_data_overlay(), er_builder_quantile_errorbar(), er_builder_quantile_errorbar_vlines(), er_builder_group_boxplot(), er_builder_summary_pvalue(). er_-namespaced shared prefix groups every builder together (discoverable via autocomplete/library(help = "erplots")) layer name stays second token, er_builder_data_*, er_builder_model_*, etc. still grep-able families. builder-metadata helper(s) custom builder can tag also moved prefix – originally three separate setters (er_builder_layout(), er_builder_fill_role(), er_builder_y_role(), renamed er_layout() two raw attr() calls, attr(builder, "er_data_fill") <- .../attr(builder, "er_group_y") <- ...), later consolidated one function, er_builder_tag(), layout/fill_role/y_role independent optional arguments (see “Extensibility” ). ci_*() – confidence-interval helpers (*_interval suffix): ci_clopper_pearson() (clopper_pearson_interval()), ci_t() (t_interval()), ci_poisson() (poisson_interval()). Chosen confint_*() avoid echoing stats::confint()’s different calling convention. Internal (dot-prefixed) helpers mostly left alone: .part_*(), .polish_*(), .get_*()/.set_*() keep existing names, internal .build_*() assembly helpers R/er-plot-build.R (e.g. .build_model_geoms()) longer risk confused public builder family now public prefix er_builder_* rather build_*.

what: Extensibility: builder is the sole mechanism (no style argument)
title: AGENTS.md
text: Every er_plot_add_*() function (er_plot_add_model(), er_plot_add_quantiles(), er_plot_add_data(), er_plot_add_groups()) takes builder argument (er_plot_add_model() additionally takes summary_builder) defaults one built-er_builder_*() function (er_builder_model_ribbonline(), er_builder_quantile_errorbar(), er_builder_data_overlay(), er_builder_group_boxplot(); summary_builder defaults er_builder_summary_pvalue()) can set function matching standard er_builder_*() signature (function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style)) – built-(e.g. er_builder_model_spaghetti(), er_builder_model_line(), er_builder_quantile_pointrange(), er_builder_quantile_bar(), er_builder_data_boxjitter(), er_builder_data_hex(), er_builder_group_violin(), er_builder_group_histogram()) fully custom (e.g. geom_crossbar()-based quantile builder, density/histogram-based data-layer builder instead scatter). used separate style string argument alongside builder, redundant three four layers (pure sugar choosing default er_builder_*() function) removed; builder now mechanism, documented ?er_partial’s “Writing builder” section. data layer specifically, one thing style used wasn’t just builder selection – picking structural family builder slotted (single call merged main panel, vs. one--panels stacked base plot) – now declared builder function via er_builder_tag(builder, layout = c("overlay", "panel")), exported helper attaches "er_builder_layout" attribute (one three independent, optional attributes er_builder_tag() can set single call – see ). er_plot_add_data() reads tag whatever builder ’s given (internal .builder_layout()) decide whether route .part_overlay() .part_data(). built-data builders already carry tag (er_builder_data_overlay(): "overlay"; er_builder_data_boxjitter(): "panel"); custom data-layer builder omits errors immediately informatively, rather silently landing wrong structural slot. chosen encoding layout builder’s return value .part_overlay()/.part_data() build different config shapes builder runs, layout knowable without calling builder – see PLAN.md’s “removing style, making builder sole mechanism” section full rationale. er_builder_tag(builder, layout = NULL, fill_role = NULL, y_role = NULL, layer = NULL) single consolidated setter four pieces builder self-declared metadata (layout/fill_role/y_role originally three separate functions – see “Naming scheme” PLAN.md’s “consolidating builder-metadata setters” section merged; layer added afterward). argument independent optional (aside layout mandatory data-layer builder specifically); builder needing one tag sets one call, e.g. er_builder_tag(fn, layout = "overlay", fill_role = "density", layer = "data"), close er_builder_data_hex() . full, worked-example version “write custom builder” (config contains per layer, use er_builder_tag()’s four arguments) lives article, vignettes/articles/extending.Rmd – see “Vignette structure” . layer (one "model", "summary", "quantile", "data", "group") checked, just stored: every er_plot_add_*() function (er_plot_add_model() checks builder "model" summary_builder "summary"; er_plot_add_quantiles() "quantile"; er_plot_add_data() "data"; er_plot_add_groups() "group") reads builder’s layer tag, one (via internal .check_builder_layer() helper), errors immediately, naming tagged actual layer, disagree – e.g. passing er_builder_quantile_errorbar er_plot_add_data() errors rather calling data layer’s config shape. Unlike layout, layer entirely optional: untagged builder (including every custom builder written layer existed) simply never checked. built-builders across five layers now carry tag.

text: quantile layer (er_plot_add_quantiles()) two base builders, er_builder_quantile_errorbar() (point + error bar, default) er_builder_quantile_pointrange(), plus _vlines variant – er_builder_quantile_errorbar_vlines()/ er_builder_quantile_pointrange_vlines() – additionally draws dotted geom_vline() every interior quantile-bin boundary (.e. every cutpoint except exposure variable’s overall min/max), common real-world exposure-response reporting idiom. _vlines variants thin wrappers around base builder (prepending single vline geom base builder’s return value), independent copies, can’t drift sync . cut_exposure_quantile() attaches n + 1 quantile cutpoints computes (excluding placebo) "breaks" attribute returned factor; .part_quantile() reads config$breaks, _vlines builders (via internal .quantile_boundary_vlines() helper) consume. er_builder_quantile_bar() (bar + error bar) removed – review wasn’t idiom shows real exposure-response reporting, unlike _vlines pattern – deprecation shim (see “Naming scheme” erplots doesn’t use shims). four quantile builders tagged er_builder_tag(fn, layer = "quantile").

Running examples real surfaced latent bug \dontrun{} silently hiding since least continuous/count generalisation work . bug. .part_model()’s .get_model_predictions() builds prediction newdata hands er_predict() exposure variable (length-300 grid) plus, stratify = TRUE, cross-join strata variable’s levels – way know covariates given fitted model’s formula actually contains, since erplots never fits models deliberately model-agnostic. fine long model’s er_predict() method can cope newdata ’s missing covariate formula (e.g. filling reference/mean level ). erglm’s current er_predict.erglm_model() : calls stats::predict(object, newdata, ...) directly fallback, predict.glm() errors (object 'sex' found model fit sex covariate) moment newdata doesn’t include every covariate formula references. bites concretely. er_plot_add_model(mod) call mod’s formula includes covariate either () isn’t plot’s stratify_by variable , (b) stratify_by, keep_strata resolves FALSE call (either explicitly, implicitly er_plot() built without stratify_by first place). Two @examples blocks er_plot_add_data()’s docs hit exactly (fixed now making sure every example plotting mod2 – fit ae2 ~ aucss + sex – always stratifies sex), underlying gap general: user fits model covariates beyond exposure variable calls er_plot_add_model(mod, keep_strata = FALSE), omits stratify_by er_plot() altogether, currently hit crash model implementation (just erglm’s) doesn’t defensively handle incomplete newdata. needs deciding, roughly priority order: 1. what: Completed: removing er_builder_quantile_bar(), adding _vlines quantile builder variants
title: erplots development plan
text: Motivation. review, er_builder_quantile_bar() (bar + error bar) didn’t earn keep built-: ’s idiom shows real exposure-response reporting. Meanwhile, drawing dotted line quantile-bin boundary – reader can see exactly one bin ends next begins without inferring point/error-bar spacing – common real-world annotation, wasn’t available built-builder. Decisions made: - er_builder_quantile_bar() removed outright (deprecation shim – rationale naming-scheme rename: GitHub-/pre-CRAN, installed user base break silently). - cut_exposure_quantile() now attaches n + 1 quantile cutpoints (excluding placebo) computes internally "breaks" attribute returned factor, rather discarding . .part_quantile() reads back exposure_bins (attributes factor column survive plain dplyr::mutate() assignment) new config$breaks field, builders can draw boundary separators without recomputing quantiles . - Two new builders, er_builder_quantile_errorbar_vlines() er_builder_quantile_pointrange_vlines(), thin wrappers around existing er_builder_quantile_errorbar()/ er_builder_quantile_pointrange() prepend single geom_vline(xintercept = <interior breaks>, linetype = "dotted") wrapped builder’s geom list (c(list(vlines), geoms) – ggplot2::ggplot_add.NULL makes NULL vline, interior boundaries draw, silent -op rather requiring special-casing). “Interior” breaks drop overall min/max, since sit /beyond panel’s edges aren’t boundaries reader need marked. Implemented wrappers, copies, two variants can’t drift builders ’re based . - new builders tagged er_builder_tag(fn, layer = "quantile"), matching every built-quantile builder. changed: R/utils-helpers.R (cut_exposure_quantile()’s "breaks" attribute), R/er-plot-part.R (.part_quantile()’s config$breaks), R/er-plot-builder-quantile.R (er_builder_quantile_bar() removed; .quantile_boundary_vlines() helper two new _vlines builders added), R/er-plot-api.R (er_plot_add_quantiles() docs/ examples), tests tests/testthat/test-er-plot-builder-quantile.R (bar tests removed, new tests config$breaks _vlines builders added) test-er-plot-part.R/test-er-plot-api.R (updated new config$breaks field er_builder_quantile_bar()’s removal), vignettes/articles/extending.Rmd (config-contents table worked example’s builder-alternatives list), NAMESPACE/man/ regenerated via devtools::document(). Status: done, devtools::check() clean (0 errors/warnings/notes), full test suite passing (471 tests).

title: erplots development plan
text: Motivation. erplots originally binary-response-three layers (quantile summary, data strip, VPC), even though model curve/ribbon group panel layers always response-type-agnostic. erglm gained gaussian/Gamma/poisson family support matching example columns (biomarker_change, ae_count, ae_duration alongside ae1/ae2), stopped hypothetical: fitting gaussian model piping er_plot_add_quantiles() silently mis-plotted (error bars pinned y = 0/1) rather erroring. Decisions made: - response_type = c("auto", "binary", "continuous", "count") er_plot()/er_vpc_plot(). "auto" detects "binary" (logical values confined {0, 1}) vs. "continuous", never resolves "count" – ’s opt-, ’s purely additive existing auto-detected behaviour. - Continuous-response quantile bins use t-interval mean (bootstrap), matching inference convention erglm uses dispersion-estimated families. - Count responses default treated "continuous" (mean + t-interval) even "auto", documented known approximation; response_type = "count" explicit opt-fast-follow swaps exact Poisson/Garwood interval (ci_poisson()), (unlike t-interval) never produces negative lower bound low-count bin. - data strip (er_plot_show_datastrip(), named ) generalised pass – binary “responders line” two-panel design direct continuous analogue, left erroring loudly continuous/count responses rather guessing design. (Superseded later – see next section.) changed: er_plot() response-type detection/storage scale-aware corner-distance placement model layer’s p-value label; .part_quantile()/er_builder_quantile_errorbar() dispatch response type (rate+Clopper-Pearson / mean+t-interval / mean+exact- Poisson); er_vpc_plot() gained dispatch observed-side summary; shared .abort_continuous_unsupported() guard added first (Stage 5) nothing silently mis-plot generalisation landed layer layer, removed call site layer generalised (now callers – see ). Status: done, devtools::check() clean, full test coverage including continuous/count fixtures (er_test_mod_gaussian, er_test_mod_poisson tests/testthat/helper-data.R), vignettes/articles/plot-count.Rmd’s “Quantile component” section (including synthetic low-count example demonstrating t-interval’s negative-lower-bound failure mode vs. exact Poisson interval’s non-negative one). (time landed, worked example lived single combined plot.Rmd, later split per-response-type articles – see “Completed: splitting plotting vignette response type” .)

title: erplots development plan
text: Motivation. pass’s “continuous variant” call data strip explicitly left open revisiting given concrete use case; section revisit. Naming decision (landed ahead redesign): “data strip” -> “data layer” throughout (er_plot_show_datastrip() -> er_plot_add_data(), .part_strip() -> .part_data(), build_datastrip_jitter() -> build_data_jitter(), object$part/plot$strip -> $data, etc.), matching existing model/quantile/group “named shows” convention. Design chosen continuous/count variant: single panel, points jittered along y = 0 , color mapped continuously response value rather partitioning upper/lower panels binary flag. Rejected alternatives: threshold-split two-panel design (rejected – threshold, e.g. median, arbitrary -motivated choice every continuous response) quantile-binned rug (rejected first pass using vertical space, noted reasonable fallback color encoding proves hard read practice). second, now-default design added alongside : rather separate panel, er_builder_data_overlay() plots raw points directly base model panel true (exposure, response) coordinates – response-type-agnostic (amount jitter differs: small vertical nudge binary 0/1 values, none otherwise), color aesthetic (stratified) always strata, sharing base plot’s legend. er_plot_add_data()’s style argument defaults "overlay"; style = "jitter" selects older panel-based design. two styles mutually exclusive (object$part$data vs. object$part$overlay). Mechanics forced open: - strip composition machinery (.build_strip_plot(), .polish_margins()/.polish_arrangement()/.polish_labels()) hardcoded exactly two named panels (upper/lower). Generalised named list zero--panels (keyed stratum level continuous/count facet fallback), panel build order/vertical position (""/"") looked rather hardcoded. - Color longer reliably means “strata” every layer. config$color_role tag ("strata" vs. "response", set .part_data()) tells .polish_labels()/.polish_legends() whether part’s legend shared, deduplicated strata legend standalone response colorbar – consulted instead inferring meaning stratify alone. - generalises one-sentence rule, now documented ?er_plot’s “Stratification” section, ?er_plot_add_data, ?er_partial, vignettes/articles/design.Rmd: layer’s encoding takes precedence; stratification adapts whatever channel left, defaulting color/fill falling back per-stratum facets color already spoken . Today data layer’s style = "jitter" path (continuous/count) needs facet fallback. Status: done – composition refactor, build_data_color() + dispatch, er_builder_data_overlay() + new default style, vignette updates (-combined plot.Rmd’s “Data component” section covered styles side side; content now lives plot-binary.Rmd/plot-continuous.Rmd/plot-count.Rmd, see “Completed: splitting plotting vignette response type” ) landed. devtools::check() clean throughout. Open questions, yet decided (flagging, blocking): - Color scale. Every builder package relies ggplot2’s default discrete hue scale explicit scale_color_*() call. build_data_color()’s continuous colorbar uses default scale_colour_gradient(), may read lower- contrast deliberately chosen scale (e.g. scale_colour_viridis_c()) – worth deliberate decision rather defaulting one. - Legibility scale. single continuous color gradient jittered 1-D strip fairly weak channel reading exact response values – inherent chosen design, bug, worth sanity check real dataset turns matter practice.

title: erplots development plan
text: Motivation. review package’s function-naming conventions flagged three inconsistencies: pipeline verb er_plot_show_*() described rendering (“show”) actually appends declarative spec; build_*() partial-builder prefix didn’t signal “pluggable strategy function” way e.g. ggplot2’s geom_*/scale_* families , read generic, collision-prone name; confidence-interval helpers (clopper_pearson_interval(), t_interval(), poisson_interval()) used inconsistent *_interval suffix instead prefix, unlike every family package. Decisions made: - er_plot_show_*() -> er_plot_add_*() (verb fix; er_plot(), er_plot_style(), er_plot_build() unaffected). - build_*() -> er_builder_*() (shared, er_-namespaced prefix, chosen per-layer prefix like er_model_*()/er_data_*() keeps “interchangeable builder-strategy functions” legible one family autocomplete/library(help =), cost slightly longer names). builder-metadata helpers moved prefix consistency: er_layout() -> er_builder_layout(); two attribute tags used set via raw attr() calls (er_data_fill, er_group_y) gained proper setter functions, er_builder_fill_role()/er_builder_y_role(), mirroring er_builder_layout()’s wrapper-function pattern instead requiring custom builder author poke attr() hand-typed string. - clopper_pearson_interval()/t_interval()/poisson_interval() -> ci_clopper_pearson()/ci_t()/ci_poisson(). ci_*() chosen confint_*() specifically avoid echoing stats::confint()’s name (generic different calling convention) despite confint_*() explicit first read. changed: every occurrence renamed across R/, tests/, vignettes/articles/, README.Rmd, AGENTS.md; NAMESPACE/man/ regenerated via devtools::document(). Internal (dot-prefixed) helpers deliberately left alone, including .build_*() assembly helpers R/er-plot-build.R – renaming public prefix away build_* incidentally resolved pre-existing ambiguity public builders, rename needed internal side. Historical mentions already-removed functions (build_data_jitter(), build_data_color(), build_datastrip_jitter()) left old names elsewhere document, since removed old naming scheme never existed new one. Straight rename, deprecation shims – package GitHub-/pre-CRAN. Status: done, devtools::check() clean (0 errors/warnings/notes), full test suite passing (452 tests).

what: Building a plot is composing layers onto an object
title: The plotting grammar
text: er_plot() creates empty object class er_plot, storing data plot’s exposure/response/stratification variables. pipe one layer functions, adds one visual component, finish plot()/print() ([er_plot_build()] directly):  currently four layers, documented help topic:
code: mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial())  erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod) |>   er_plot_add_quantiles() |>   er_plot_add_groups(aucss) |>   plot() observed data       |       v   er_plot()                       -- creates the (empty) object       |       v   layer functions (piped, any order, any subset):     er_plot_add_model()     er_plot_add_quantiles()     er_plot_add_data()     er_plot_add_groups()       |       v   er_plot_build()                 -- called for you by plot()/print()       |       v   polish: margins, labels, legends, theme       |       v   compose: patchwork       |       v   rendered plot

what: Layers are either singleton or additive
title: The plotting grammar
text: Calling layer function twice object doesn’t always thing. model, quantile, data layers singleton: second call replaces first call’s result rather combining two. group layer one exception: ’s additive. call adds another panel alongside already added, rather replacing : deliberate design choice, oversight: one “model” one “quantile summary” show per plot, many legitimate ways slice exposure distribution different grouping variables. one flagged exception watch : model layer singleton, overlaying two model curves comparison (e.g. candidate model reference model) isn’t currently possible. ’s tracked plausible future addition, planned work – see PLAN.md.
code: plt <- erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_add_quantiles(bins = 4) |>   er_plot_add_quantiles(bins = 8) # overwrites the bins = 4 call  plt$part$quantile$config$n_quantiles # 8, not 4 #> [1] 8 plt <- erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_add_groups(aucss) |>   er_plot_add_groups(treatment) # adds a second panel, doesn't replace the first  names(plt$part$group$config) # both grouping variables are still there #> [1] "treatment"

what: Stratification composes with layers, usually via color
title: The plotting grammar
text: stratify_by, set er_plot(), declares single discrete variable used split layers color/fill, one shared, deduplicated legend across whole composed plot:  layer’s keep_strata argument controls whether layer uses stratification (default TRUE whenever stratify_by set). general rule, order layer actually applies : layer’s encoding takes precedence; stratification adapts whatever channel left, defaulting color/fill. layers, color/fill always free strata, rule invisible practice. data layer one exception, behaviour now depends builder play, structural family (declared via [er_builder_tag()]) builder belongs : er_builder_data_overlay() (default, "overlay"-layout): color, mapped , always means strata – response already shown via y-position, color/fill free stratification like every layer, overlay shares base plot’s strata legend model/quantile layers. er_builder_data_boxjitter() (older, panel-based design, "panel"-layout, binary-response ): behaves way overlay – color/fill means strata, shared legend. built-"panel"-layout builder continuous/count response today; one written, color aesthetic typically already spoken response value (removed build_data_color() builder’s ), case stratification fall back one panel per stratum level instead shared legend – concrete instance “layer’s encoding takes precedence” motivated general rule. See PLAN.md’s “Continuous-response data strip” section design history, [er_plot_add_data()] full breakdown. config$color_role tag ("strata" "response", set .part_data()) records meaning applies given data-layer build, composition machinery (.polish_labels()/ .polish_legends()) knows whether treat builder’s legend shared strata legend standalone response colorbar.
code: mod_strat <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial())  erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1, stratify_by = sex) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod_strat) |>   er_plot_add_quantiles() |>   er_plot_add_data() |>   plot()

what: Response type changes what a layer summarises, not whether it appears
title: The plotting grammar
text: response_type, also set er_plot() ("auto", "binary", "continuous", "count"), governs response’s scale summary/CI method response-type-aware layer uses. model group layers don’t look response’s type – consume [er_predict()]/[er_simulate()] output exposure variable, respectively. quantile layer dispatches directly: "auto" classifies response "binary" ’s logical confined {0, 1}, "continuous" otherwise – genuine count response auto-detects "continuous" summarised approximately-continuous quantity unless response_type = "count" declared explicitly. See [er_plot_add_quantiles()] full rationale count responses article’s “Quantile component” section worked example, including case choice t-interval exact Poisson interval visibly matters. data layer doesn’t compute summary statistic – just plots raw observations – response_type instead changes ’s drawn: er_builder_data_overlay() (default) needs dispatch (plain scatter, small vertical jitter binary response’s exactly-0/1 y-values); er_builder_data_boxjitter() binary-response , uses response_type insofar er_plot_add_data() guards using continuous/count response (’s upper/lower partition split ) – see [er_plot_add_data()].

title: Extending erplots: writing your own builder

Concretely:

what: Worked example: a custom quantile builder
title: Extending erplots: writing your own builder
text: Suppose built-er_builder_quantile_errorbar() (point + error bar) er_builder_quantile_pointrange() alternatives (bin-boundary-annotated _vlines variants) feel like wrong idiom, ’d rather draw per-bin summary geom_crossbar(). code: attr(er_builder_data_overlay, "er_builder_layout") #> [1] "overlay" attr(er_builder_data_boxjitter, "er_builder_layout") #> [1] "panel" untagged_builder <- function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style) {   ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[exposure$name]], y = .data[[response$name]])) }  erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_add_data(builder = untagged_builder) |>   plot() #> Error in `.builder_layout()`: #> ! `builder` must declare its structural layout. #> ℹ Wrap a custom data-layer builder with `er_builder_tag(builder, layout = "overlay")` or `er_builder_tag(builder, layout = "panel")`. #> ℹ The built-in builders (`er_builder_data_overlay()`, `er_builder_data_boxjitter()`) already do this. er_builder_data_density <- er_builder_tag(   function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style) {     ggplot2::geom_density2d(       data = data,       mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[exposure$name]], y = .data[[response$name]]),       inherit.aes = FALSE     )   },   layout = "overlay" )  erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod) |>   er_plot_add_data(builder = er_builder_data_density) |>   plot()

text: auto-detection, count response summarised way continuous response – bin mean plus t-interval:  Declaring response_type = "count" swaps t-interval approximation exact Poisson interval (bin mean plus [ci_poisson()] instead [ci_t()]), never produces negative lower bound – useful low-count bins, t-interval approximation can. erglm_data’s ae_count, none bin means low enough actually happen, two plots look almost identical re-ran last one response_type = "count". make difference concrete, ’s synthetic dataset placebo arm 2 events among 20 subjects: default (t-interval) path’s placebo-arm error bar dips visibly zero – nonsensical negative event rate:   Declaring response_type = "count" keeps placebo-arm point estimate replaces t-interval exact Poisson interval, stays non-negative:
code: erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae_count) |>    er_plot_add_model(mod_poisson) |>    er_plot_add_quantiles() |>    plot() set.seed(84) placebo_counts <- rpois(20, 0.05) while (sum(placebo_counts) != 2) placebo_counts <- rpois(20, 0.05)  aucss_dosed <- sort(runif(80, 100, 3000)) count_dosed <- rpois(80, 0.1 + 0.003 * aucss_dosed)  low_count_data <- data.frame(   aucss = c(rep(0, 20), aucss_dosed),   ae_count = c(placebo_counts, count_dosed) )  mod_low_count <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss, low_count_data, family = poisson()) low_count_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae_count) |>    er_plot_add_model(mod_low_count) |>    er_plot_add_quantiles() |>    plot() +   ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 1.5), xlim = c(-50, 800)) #> Coordinate system already present. #> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one. low_count_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae_count, response_type = "count") |>    er_plot_add_model(mod_low_count) |>    er_plot_add_quantiles() |>    plot() +   ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 1.5), xlim = c(-50, 800)) #> Coordinate system already present. #> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one.

title: Model-Agnostic Exposure-Response Plots
text: erplots provides fluent mini-language building exposure-response plots: model curves/ribbons, quantile-binned response-rate summaries, data strips, grouped distribution panels. model-agnostic: erplots never fits model . Instead, fit model whatever package suits workflow (e.g. erglm logistic regression), pass fitted object er_plot_add_model(). model implements er_predict() can visualised; implementing er_simulate() er_summary() additionally enables uncertainty spaghetti plots/VPCs summary annotations (e.g. p-values). See ?er_model_interface.

text: Every er_builder_*() function shares signature documented @params, signature public part API, implementation detail: function function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style) returns geom list geoms can stand built-builder. officially supported way draw layer differently built-builder options – e.g. 2D density instead scatter data overlay, per-panel histograms instead jittered points panel-based data layer, geom_crossbar() instead geom_errorbar()/geom_pointrange() quantile summary. (er_builder_quantile_pointrange() started life exactly kind custom builder – promoted built-option proved natural, low-risk alternative er_builder_quantile_errorbar(), new config requirements.) er_plot_show_*() function takes builder argument (er_plot_add_model() additionally takes summary_builder) defaults one built-er_builder_*() function can set – built-custom – matching standard signature, string-based style argument : custom builder can plugged without forking package reaching object$part internals. data layer specifically, builder also declare structural family belongs – single call merged main panel, one panels stacked base plot – via er_builder_tag(), since er_plot_add_data() reads tag builder decide assemble layer; three layers one structural call site, tagging needed . See @examples er_plot_add_model(), er_plot_add_quantiles(), er_plot_add_data() worked custom builders (dashed model curve, quantile crossbar, data-overlay density, respectively). custom builder receives pre-computed config built-builder received layer (e.g. config$predictions model, config$summary quantile) – need recompute anything corresponding .part_*() function already derived data/exposure/response/strata; needs turn config ggplot2 layers. custom builder can optionally self-declare layer meant via er_builder_tag(builder, layer = ...) (one "model", "summary", "quantile", "data", "group"). Every er_plot_add_*() function checks builder's layer tag, one, layer actually passed , erroring immediately disagree – e.g. passing builder tagged layer = "quantile" er_plot_add_data() errors rather calling builder config shape written . tag entirely optional (unlike layout, mandatory data-layer builder specifically) – untagged custom builder simply never checked, existing custom builders keep working unchanged. built-builders carry tag. builders feed singleton layer: model, summary, quantile, data, overlay occupy single named slot (object$part$model, object$part$data, etc.), calling corresponding er_plot_show_*() function overwrites slot rather combining builders. group (er_builder_group_boxplot()/ er_builder_group_violin()) one additive exception – call er_plot_add_groups() adds another named entry rather replacing previous one. See er_plot()'s "Layers either singleton additive" section full discussion, including one flagged future exception (additive model layer, overlaying two fitted curves). data slot's default, er_builder_data_overlay(), needs color_role tag: color aesthetic (stratified) always strata, since response already shown via y-position, shares base plot's strata legend directly. config$color_role (set .part_data(), consulted .polish_labels()/ .polish_legends() R/er-plot-compose.R) matters "panel"-layout family instead, "strata" binary response (used built-er_builder_data_boxjitter(), whose color aesthetic still means strata) "response" continuous/count response, color channel already spoken response value – built-"panel"-layout builder case (older build_data_color() removed er_builder_data_overlay() covered typical use case simply), custom builder tagged er_builder_tag(builder, layout = "panel") can still opt ; see er_plot_add_data() user-facing version rule.

Governs response-scale defaults (e.g. axis limits) summary/CI method quantile VPC layers use. "auto", response column classified "binary" logical takes values {0, 1}, "continuous" otherwise – means count (Poisson-style) response, e.g. adverse-event count, auto-detects "continuous" (counts confined {0, 1}) summarised approximately-continuous quantity (bin mean plus t-interval). "auto" never resolves "count": pass response_type = "count" explicitly genuine count response instead get bin mean plus exact Poisson interval (see ci_poisson()), – unlike t-interval approximation – never produces negative lower bound. what: Layers are either singleton or additive
title: The exposure-response plotting mini-language — er_plot
text: er_plot_add_model(), er_plot_add_quantiles(), er_plot_add_data() singleton: calling one twice object overwrites first call's result rather combining two. er_plot_add_groups() additive: call adds another grouped-distribution panel alongside already added, rather replacing . asymmetry deliberate, accidental – one "model" one "quantile summary" show per plot, many legitimate ways slice exposure distribution different grouping variables. See PLAN.md's "Mini-language architecture review" design discussion, including one flagged future exception: overlaying two model curves comparison currently supported, one singleton layer additive variant might eventually make sense.

title: The exposure-response plotting mini-language — er_plot
text: stratify_by (set , er_plot()) declares single discrete variable used split layers color/fill, one shared, deduplicated legend across whole composed plot. layer function's keep_strata argument controls whether layer actually uses stratification (defaults TRUE whenever stratify_by set, FALSE otherwise). er_plot_add_data() partial exception "always color/fill" rule continuous/count response: color aesthetic already spoken response value , stratification falls back one panel per stratum level instead shared legend – see documentation PLAN.md general "layer's encoding takes precedence" rule follows.

title: Add a raw-data layer — er_plot_add_data
text: Adds data layer: individual observations. default (builder = er_builder_data_overlay), points drawn true (exposure, response) coordinates main model panel – plain scatter continuous/count responses, scatter small vertical jitter binary response (whose y-values exactly 0/1 otherwise overplot two solid lines). works uniformly across three response types, response-type dispatch builder use. er_builder_data_boxjitter() instead uses older, panel-based design, binary-response-: responders (response == 1) get boxplot + jittered points upper panel non-responders (response == 0) get lower panel, panel shows exposure distribution conditional response, just raw points. built-"panel"-layout builder continuous/count response – older build_data_color() (single panel points colored continuously response value) removed er_builder_data_overlay() turned cover typical use case simply; panel must "" (default) response types regardless builder, since upper/lower partition select .

text: object Partially constructed plot (S3 class er_plot) keep_strata Logical, indicating whether layer split plot's stratification variable; defaults TRUE stratify_by set er_plot(), FALSE otherwise. "panel"-layout builder continuous/count response produces one panel per stratum level (see "Stratification" ) rather shared color aesthetic; "overlay"-layout builder always means shared color aesthetic, response type. builder Function drawing data layer – defaults er_builder_data_overlay(). er_builder_data_boxjitter() (binary response : boxplot + jittered points per panel) built-option; function matching standard (data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style) signature tagged er_builder_tag() can supplied instead – see er_builder() full contract, e.g. 2D density main panel, continuous/ count response's color-encoded panel, per-panel histograms. builder tagged layer "data", errors informatively; untagged builder never checked (layout hard requirement). panel Character string: "upper", "lower", "" (default). meaningful er_builder_data_boxjitter() binary response; must "" "overlay"-layout builder (upper/lower partition exists) continuous/count response "panel"-layout builder (upper/lower partition select either way).

title: Add a raw-data layer — er_plot_add_data
text: Every data-layer builder declares two structural families belongs via er_builder_tag() – "overlay" (single call merged main panel) "panel" (one--panels stacked base plot) – er_plot_add_data() reads builder decide assemble layer, rather taking separate argument . makes pairing structural rather incidental: er_builder_data_overlay() can never routed upper/lower panels, er_builder_data_boxjitter() can never merged main panel. See er_builder_tag() er_builder() tag custom builder way. layer singleton – see er_plot()'s "Layers either singleton additive" – calling (builder) fully replaces previous data layer. "panel"-layout builder also one case stratification behaviour partial exception "always color/fill" (see er_plot()'s "Stratification" section): continuous/count response, color aesthetic already spoken response value, stratification instead produces one panel per stratum level (stacked base plot, colored response), rather shared strata legend. "overlay"-layout builder exception – color aesthetic (stratified) always strata, since response already shown via y-position, shares base plot's strata legend (one model/quantile layers use) rather needing one .

title: Add a raw-data layer — er_plot_add_data
code: if (requireNamespace("erglm", quietly = TRUE)) { library(erglm) mod2 <- erglm_model(ae2 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae2, stratify_by = sex) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod2) |>   er_plot_add_quantiles() |>   er_plot_add_data() |>   plot()  # continuous response: overlay works the same way, with no # response-type-specific styling needed mod3 <- erglm_model(biomarker_change ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = gaussian()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, biomarker_change) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod3) |>   er_plot_add_data() |>   plot()  # older panel-based design, binary-response only: a boxplot + jittered # points per panel (responders above, non-responders below), instead # of an overlay in the main panel erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae2, stratify_by = sex) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod2) |>   er_plot_add_data(builder = er_builder_data_boxjitter) |>   plot()  # plug in a 2D density in the main panel instead of a scatter; tagging # it "overlay" via `er_builder_tag()` keeps it in the single main-panel # layout -- see `?er_builder` build_data_density <- er_builder_tag(   function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style) {     ggplot2::geom_density_2d(       data = data,       mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[exposure$name]], y = .data[[response$name]])     )   },   layout = "overlay" ) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, biomarker_change) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod3) |>   er_plot_add_data(builder = build_data_density) |>   plot() }

code: if (requireNamespace("erglm", quietly = TRUE)) { library(erglm) mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod) |>   plot()  # a spaghetti plot instead of the default ribbon erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod, builder = er_builder_model_spaghetti) |>   plot()  # plug in a fully custom model-curve builder; see `?er_builder` for the # full contract build_model_dashed <- function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style) {   ggplot2::geom_line(     data = config$predictions,     mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = .data[[exposure$name]], y = fit_resp),     linetype = "dashed"   ) } erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod, builder = build_model_dashed) |>   plot() }  #> Using seed = 4188

title: Add a quantile-binned response summary layer — er_plot_add_quantiles
text: Count responses auto-detect "continuous" (see er_plot()'s response_type parameter) summarised way continuous response unless response_type = "count" declared explicitly. layer singleton – see er_plot()'s "Layers either singleton additive" – calling twice replaces previous quantile summary rather combining bins calls.

title: Add a quantile-binned response summary layer — er_plot_add_quantiles
code: if (requireNamespace("erglm", quietly = TRUE)) { library(erglm) mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod) |>   er_plot_add_quantiles() |>   plot()  # continuous response: bin means/t-intervals instead of rates/ # Clopper-Pearson intervals, auto-detected from the response column mod3 <- erglm_model(biomarker_change ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = gaussian()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, biomarker_change) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod3) |>   er_plot_add_quantiles() |>   plot()  # count response: declare response_type = "count" explicitly for an # exact Poisson interval instead of the t-interval approximation used # by the auto-detected ("continuous") default mod4 <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = poisson()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae_count, response_type = "count") |>   er_plot_add_model(mod4) |>   er_plot_add_quantiles() |>   plot()  # a pointrange instead of the default errorbar erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod) |>   er_plot_add_quantiles(builder = er_builder_quantile_pointrange) |>   plot()  # the default errorbar, with dotted lines marking the quantile-bin # boundaries erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod) |>   er_plot_add_quantiles(builder = er_builder_quantile_errorbar_vlines) |>   plot()  # plug in a fully custom builder; see `?er_builder` for the full contract build_quantile_crossbar <- function(data, config, stratify, exposure, response, strata, style) {   ggplot2::geom_crossbar(     data = config$summary,     mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = x_mid, y = y_mid, ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper),     inherit.aes = FALSE   ) } erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_add_model(mod) |>   er_plot_add_quantiles(builder = build_quantile_crossbar) |>   plot() }

what: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
text: Compares observed response rates simulated response rates model, stratified grouping variable. function model-agnostic: operates purely data frames. sim data frame expected contain one row per simulated observation per replicate, sim_id column identifying replicate (see e.g. erglm::erglm_vpc_sim() one way generate simulations fitted model).

title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
code: er_vpc_plot(   data,   sim,   exposure,   response,   group_by,   conf_level = 0.95,   response_type = c("auto", "binary", "continuous", "count") )

title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
text: data Observed data sim Simulated data, exposure/response/group_by columns data, plus sim_id column identifying replicate exposure Exposure variable (one variable, unquoted) response Response variable (one variable, unquoted). May binary (0/1, logical) continuous; see response_type group_by Variable (unquoted) stratify predictions conf_level Confidence level response_type One "auto" (default), "binary", "continuous", "count". Governs observed-side summary computed: response rate Clopper-Pearson CI "binary", bin mean t-interval "continuous" (see ci_t()), bin mean exact Poisson interval "count" (see ci_poisson()). "auto" detects observed response column (entirely {0, 1}, logical, treated binary; see er_plot()'s response_type heuristic) never resolves "count": count (Poisson-style) response auto-detects "continuous" (counts confined {0, 1}) summarised bin-mean-plus-t-interval approximation unless response_type = "count" declared explicitly, case exact Poisson interval used instead – see PLAN.md's design decision (4) rationale.

title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
code: if (requireNamespace("erglm", quietly = TRUE)) { library(erglm) mod <- erglm_model(ae2 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial()) sim <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod) er_vpc_plot(erglm_data, sim, aucss, ae2, group_by = aucss) er_vpc_plot(erglm_data, sim, aucss, ae2, group_by = sex)  mod_gaussian <- erglm_model(biomarker_change ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = gaussian()) sim_gaussian <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod_gaussian) er_vpc_plot(erglm_data, sim_gaussian, aucss, biomarker_change, group_by = aucss)  mod_poisson <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = poisson()) sim_poisson <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod_poisson) er_vpc_plot(   erglm_data, sim_poisson, aucss, ae_count, group_by = aucss,   response_type = "count" ) } #> Using seed = 4289 #> Using seed = 5993 #> Using seed = 9248
